purpose: Benchmark RPG.kr job-lineage design patterns without copying exact data.
numericPolicy: No exact AP, chance, mastery, stat thresholds, coefficients, or percent bonuses are stored.

lineages.role: starter survival and shared utility base
lineages.coreIdentity: A foundation route that gives early survival tools and opens many later branches.
signatureMechanics: Basic shove, grit, emergency survival, and small recovery tools.
Works as a hub before entering physical, magic, ranged, or eastern routes.
Good benchmark for tutorial jobs that are useful without defining a final build.
benchmarkIdeas: Early job that grants one universal survival passive.
Branch-key job that unlocks several later families.
Low damage, high stability, broad compatibility.

lineages.coreIdentity: A fast, evasive utility route that rewards dodging, stealth, and weak-point attacks.
signatureMechanics: Evasion and turn-tempo tools define the early identity.
Critical chance and ambush behavior make it useful as a side route.
Can bridge into assassin or speed-based builds.
benchmarkIdeas: A dodge action that also shortens the next action delay.
Ambush bonus when enemy HP is high or enemy is marked.
Passive crit chance from stealth discipline.

lineages.coreIdentity: An undead military-rank progression route, good for attrition, dark durability, and branching rulers.
signatureMechanics: Rank escalation creates a clear ladder from weak undead to commanders.
Fits HP, physical attack, and physical defense growth themes.
Can split into warrior skeletons, wandering skeletons, kings, or overlords.
benchmarkIdeas: Undead route that improves through command-rank titles.
Low finesse but high persistence and attrition.
Mastery can unlock ruler-style branch jobs.

lineages.coreIdentity: A bucket for special fantasy identities that do not fit a single primary stat family.
signatureMechanics: Hybrid physical/magic routes are appropriate here.
Celestial, fallen, despair, berserk, and sword-sage themes can live here.
Works best as special unlocks rather than a tightly linear family.
benchmarkIdeas: Rare branch job that changes build rules.
Mixed PA/MA scaling job.
Story-flavored special class with unusual tradeoffs.

lineages.coreIdentity: A durable frontline route that turns defense into offense and uses absolute or defense-ignoring damage with low critical reliance.
signatureMechanics: PD and MD can directly improve attack performance.
Absolute damage and defense-ignoring damage fit the family.
Criticals can be disabled, ignored, or made less important.
Defensive passives and basic-attack conversions define the progression.
Dragon knight or holy knight branches are natural extensions.
benchmarkIdeas: Damage scales from defense instead of attack.
Cannot crit, but ignores enemy defense.
Builds guard stacks, then converts them into a heavy strike.
Wins long fights through reliable fixed damage.

lineages.coreIdentity: A knight-derived route that mixes frontline durability with holy judgment, apocalypse themes, and enemy debuffs.
signatureMechanics: Holy knight side emphasizes protection, judgment, and balanced defense.
Apocalypse knight side emphasizes death, plague, war, famine, and negative enemy states.
Works well when enemy debuffs feed finishing damage.
Can sit between tank, debuffer, and executioner.
benchmarkIdeas: Judgment skill that becomes stronger on weakened enemies.
Debuff aura that lowers enemy defense before execution.
Apocalypse branch where each job represents a different combat pressure.

lineages.coreIdentity: A burst route that exploits negative enemy defense/evasion, critical stats, marks, and delay tradeoffs.
signatureMechanics: Enemy AV or PD below zero can unlock bonus damage.
CT and CD are highly relevant.
Large attacks can create self-delay or recoil.
Marks, back attacks, and prior setup are good activation conditions.
Pairs well with allies or jobs that shred enemy defense/evasion first.
benchmarkIdeas: Extra damage if enemy evasion or defense is negative.
Assassination skill scales with critical damage.
Big strike followed by delayed next turn.
Execution only against marked targets.

lineages.coreIdentity: A martial route that lowers enemy defense/evasion and grows through realm-like progression stages.
signatureMechanics: Repeated attacks can shred enemy PD and AV.
Early jobs are modest, but higher realms unlock stronger stances or attack conversions.
Late power can depend heavily on critical support.
Realm, inner-power, outer-power, and stance themes fit well.
benchmarkIdeas: Each hit applies a small defense/evasion shred.
Realm upgrades change the default attack behavior.
Strong in longer fights where debuffs have time to accumulate.

lineages.coreIdentity: A fallen martial route that trades stability for violent burst and high critical dependency.
signatureMechanics: Dark martial flavor should feel more explosive and less stable than normal martial jobs.
Critical support is important before the route reaches full value.
Works as a late-game route after mastering both orthodox and fallen martial identities.
benchmarkIdeas: Critical burst increases as enemy debuffs stack.
Dark stance with self-risk but higher finishing power.
Final class that combines martial shreds with critical nukes.

lineages.role: speed and critical ranged damage dealer
lineages.coreIdentity: A ranged route based on speed, accuracy, critical chance, critical damage, and frequent actions.
signatureMechanics: Action delay reduction and fast tempo are core.
CT and CD improve damage efficiency.
Fragile body, high output, and short fight windows fit the route.
Late jobs can pivot into burst, absolute damage, or defense-ignoring shots.
benchmarkIdeas: Ranged damage boosted by critical chance.
Charge or reload state after several attacks.
Fast but fragile glass-cannon identity.

lineages.role: opening burst dark ranged damage dealer
lineages.coreIdentity: A dark ranged route that trades HP or defense for high CT/CD and strong early-fight burst.
signatureMechanics: Self-penalty in exchange for critical power.
Limited-use or opening-window attacks fit well.
Defense debuffs plus ranged burst are a strong identity.
benchmarkIdeas: Spend HP to force a critical shot.
Powerful shot only near the start of battle.
Weak durability compensated by high crit damage.

lineages.role: time and delay manipulation ranged support
lineages.coreIdentity: A ranged utility route that supports AGI builds through delay reduction, turn push, time rewind, and critical-damage passives.
signatureMechanics: Manipulates combat order rather than only increasing raw speed.
Can refund self time or delay enemy turns.
Critical-damage passives make it attractive as a side route for archers.
benchmarkIdeas: After attacking, refund part of the user's action time.
Shot that delays the enemy's next turn.
Passive route for critical-damage scaling.

lineages.coreIdentity: An elemental magic route where fire, ice, earth, and lightning each imply different combat jobs.
signatureMechanics: Fire fits growth-style pure damage.
Ice fits enemy MD/AV weakening and next-hit setup.
Earth fits turn push, control, and disabling value.
Lightning fits high damage and accuracy/critical bonuses with recoil.
MA growth passives form the route backbone.
benchmarkIdeas: Split elements into damage, debuff, control, and recoil roles.
Early reliable spell that later gets replaced by specialized magic.
Late mages provide defense reduction and utility, not only bigger numbers.

lineages.coreIdentity: A dark magic route built around curses, forbidden power, summons, and risky late-game scaling.
signatureMechanics: Curses and dark stacks can amplify later damage.
Self-risk or instability distinguishes it from normal elemental magic.
Summon or forbidden-entity flavor fits the higher jobs.
benchmarkIdeas: Dark stacks increase damage but also increase danger.
Summoned entity adds attacks while draining resources.
Curse route that weakens both sides but favors the caster.

lineages.coreIdentity: A holy route focused on healing, protection, survival triggers, judgment, and miracle-like saves.
signatureMechanics: Low-HP triggers for healing or protection are appropriate.
Holy damage can coexist with support utility.
Last-stand, blessing, barrier, and miracle themes fit well.
benchmarkIdeas: Auto-heal in critical situations.
Attack that also heals a portion.
Holy barrier that reduces the next incoming hit.

lineages.coreIdentity: A daoist-flavored magic route suited to buffs, spirit arts, time tricks, and immortal progression.
signatureMechanics: Buffs and setup effects can matter more than direct damage.
Time manipulation and spirit-themed skills fit the route.
Immortal-stage progression provides a natural high-tier fantasy.
benchmarkIdeas: Temporary talisman buff that changes the next action.
Small time rewind or action-speed manipulation.
Spirit body or immortal form as a late upgrade.

lineages.role: curse, deception, and demon control mage
lineages.coreIdentity: A corrupted daoist route focused on curses, deception, demon power, and control effects.
signatureMechanics: Control and debuff effects should be more important than raw spell spam.
Demonization or corruption can trade safety for stronger control.
Fits enemy weakening, turn disruption, and curse stacking.
benchmarkIdeas: Curse that worsens when the enemy acts.
Illusion skill that causes enemy miss or self-delay.
Demon stance with stronger spells and defensive penalty.

lineages.coreIdentity: A set of nonstandard magic routes for spirits, summoning, destruction, and transformation.
signatureMechanics: Summoners can shift power into companions or spirits.
Destruction magic can emphasize raw spell damage with drawbacks.
Transformation magic can change stat use or available actions.
benchmarkIdeas: Spirit companion that changes based on equipped element.
Destructive spell with self-delay or self-damage.
Transformation state that swaps MA scaling into another stat.

lineages.coreIdentity: A technical side route for tracking, hunting, field control, and beast-related utility.
signatureMechanics: Works well with mark, trap, tracking, and monster-knowledge effects.
Can support physical and ranged jobs without being pure archer.
Field utility should matter as much as direct damage.
benchmarkIdeas: Mark prey to improve follow-up attacks.
Trap that delays or weakens the enemy.
Bonus against beasts or previously studied monsters.

lineages.coreIdentity: A technical side route that turns preparation, food, and crafting into combat support.
signatureMechanics: Pre-battle preparation can create temporary buffs.
Food effects can cover sustain, stat buffs, or special utility.
Works best as a support route that improves many builds.
benchmarkIdeas: Cooked meal grants a temporary combat modifier.
Ingredient quality changes the buff type, not exact copied values.
Support route that rewards preparation before difficult fights.

lineages.coreIdentity: A group of visible but unfinished jobs, useful only as future-theme placeholders.
signatureMechanics: Do not treat as reliable gameplay data.
Useful for spotting planned fantasy themes such as lower god, lower angel, undead, or skeleton mage.
Should be marked incomplete in any derived design database.
benchmarkIdeas: Keep planned jobs separate from implemented balance references.
Use as inspiration for future silhouettes, not mechanics.
Add an explicit incomplete flag in tools.
